topic: bangladesh pacers dominate australia in odi
headline_marked: [[Australia lose 3 wickets for 0 runs]] for first time in ODI history
headline: Australia lose 3 wickets for 0 runs for first time in ODI history
summary: Bangladesh bowlers Taskin Ahmed and Mustafizur Rahman ripped through Australia's top order, causing the visitors to lose three wickets without scoring a single run for the first time in One Day International history.
details: In a historic display of bowling prowess, Bangladesh achieved an unprecedented feat against Australia in the second ODI at Mirpur. The Australian team lost three of their frontline batters without adding any runs to the scoreboard, a first in ODI cricket.

The collapse began with opener Matthew Short, who was dismissed by Taskin Ahmed after surviving three deliveries. Mustafizur Rahman then took center stage in the second over, claiming the wickets of Cooper Connolly and Matt Renshaw, both without the team scoring a run.

This embarrassing milestone marks the first time Australia has recorded three wickets for zero runs in an ODI match. Historically, only three other instances exist in ODI cricket where a team lost three wickets without scoring.

These previous occurrences include Bangladesh's own struggle against Sri Lanka in the 2003 Cricket World Cup, Pakistan's fate against South Africa in 1997, and another instance of Pakistan against New Zealand in 1983.
caption:
Australia has set an unwanted record in ODI cricket, losing three wickets for zero runs for the first time ever. Bangladesh bowlers Taskin Ahmed and Mustafizur Rahman were instrumental in the historic collapse.

In a historic display of bowling prowess, Bangladesh achieved an unprecedented feat against Australia in the second ODI at Mirpur. The Australian team lost three of their frontline batters without adding any runs to the scoreboard, a first in ODI cricket.

The collapse began with opener Matthew Short, who was dismissed by Taskin Ahmed after surviving three deliveries. Mustafizur Rahman then took center stage in the second over, claiming the wickets of Cooper Connolly and Matt Renshaw, both without the team scoring a run.

This embarrassing milestone marks the first time Australia has recorded three wickets for zero runs in an ODI match. Historically, only three other instances exist in ODI cricket where a team lost three wickets without scoring.

These previous occurrences include Bangladesh's own struggle against Sri Lanka in the 2003 Cricket World Cup, Pakistan's fate against South Africa in 1997, and another instance of Pakistan against New Zealand in 1983.

#Cricket #BANvAUS #ODI #BangladeshCricket #RecordBreakers

Source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, The Daily Star
tweet: Australia makes unwanted ODI history, losing 3 wickets for 0 runs for the first time ever. Bangladesh bowlers Taskin Ahmed & Mustafizur Rahman led the charge. #Cricket #BANvAUS

topic: new pay scale for govt employees from july 1
headline_marked: Govt announces new pay scale for employees, [[implementation to be phased]] starting July 1
headline: Govt announces new pay scale for employees, implementation to be phased starting July 1
summary: Public sector employees will see a new pay scale implemented from July 1, after 11 years. The phased rollout is expected to take three fiscal years to complete.
details: The government is set to introduce a new pay scale for its officers and employees, effective July 1. Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury is expected to announce this significant change during the national budget presentation.
This marks the first revision of the pay scale for public employees in 11 years. However, the implementation will not be immediate, with a phased approach planned over three fiscal years.

Approximately 50 percent of the revised basic salary is anticipated to be disbursed in the upcoming fiscal year. To support this initiative, the government has allocated Tk 141,434 crore for public services, a substantial increase from the current fiscal year's budget.

This allocation represents a nearly 96 percent jump from the original budget of Tk 72,024 crore and an increase of about Tk 55,000 crore compared to the revised figure. The funds will also cover new recruitments, increased pay for MPO-listed teachers and staff, and additional benefits for pensioners.
caption:
Government employees to receive a new pay scale starting July 1.
Implementation will be phased over three years.

The government is set to introduce a new pay scale for its officers and employees, effective July 1. Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury is expected to announce this significant change during the national budget presentation.

This marks the first revision of the pay scale for public employees in 11 years. However, the implementation will not be immediate, with a phased approach planned over three fiscal years.

Approximately 50 percent of the revised basic salary is anticipated to be disbursed in the upcoming fiscal year. To support this initiative, the government has allocated Tk 141,434 crore for public services, a substantial increase from the current fiscal year's budget.

This allocation represents a nearly 96 percent jump from the original budget of Tk 72,024 crore and an increase of about Tk 55,000 crore compared to the revised figure. The funds will also cover new recruitments, increased pay for MPO-listed teachers and staff, and additional benefits for pensioners.
